category_title: Nonverbal elementary student with ASD who exhibits aggressive behaviors and elopement during nonpreferred activities
minimal_context: The student and educator are working one-on-one at the classroom workspace to order numbers 1 to 10.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our one-on-one work. Let's sit at the workspace and order the numbers 1 to 10.

STUDENT: (Screams and falls to the floor.)

TUTOR: Please stand up and look at the numbers.

STUDENT: (Kicks from the floor.)

TUTOR: We need to finish ordering the numbers.

STUDENT: (Hits and attempts to head-butt to protest the nonpreferred activity.)

options.A: Conduct functional behavior analyses targeting the purported variables and subject them to data-based hypothesis testing.
options.B: Remain available without forcing interaction, first simply sitting near the student and then speaking quietly and gently touching his shoulder.
options.C: Use a two-pocket folder where the left side holds numbers to be ordered and the right holds completed work.
options.D: Complete consulting notes on a daily basis to share information and strategies with other assigned educators.
